scope: Runtime smoke test of the Rust autocomplete broker from outside the process, first with the mock provider and then with a bounded real pi provider call using synthetic TypeScript and Markdown contexts.

summary.result: Broker runtime smoke passed for mock and real pi-backed requests. Rust and client tests pass. Real pi completed one TypeScript and one Markdown request through the broker using the installed openai-codex/gpt-5.5 model path, with external HTTP wall-clock median 5391.979 ms and nearest-rank p95 6254.653 ms over two successful samples.
important_notes: The plan/default model path openai/gpt-5.5 failed on this host because pi reported no OpenAI API key. The installed pi model list showed openai-codex/gpt-5.5, and broker config with pi.provider=openai-codex plus pi.model=gpt-5.5 succeeded.
Measurements use synthetic prompt-only contexts and do not prove daily-use quality, GUI editor rendering, or keystroke-level latency.
Real pi latencies of roughly 4.5 s and 6.3 s are acceptable only for bounded smoke/manual-or-idle MVP behavior, not live per-keystroke autocomplete.

commands_run.result: completed before report write. Status showed pre-existing unrelated deleted legacy reports/orchestration/* paths and untracked orchestration/archive paths; these were preserved.

passed. Full Rust workspace formatting check and tests passed for autocomplete-addons, autocomplete-core, autocomplete-protocol, autocomplete-provider-pi, and autocomplete-server, including doc tests.
passed. 9 Node tests passed across shared protocol, VS Code host glue, Obsidian host glue, cancellation, and client/provider boundary checks.
passed. Dev workspace build finished successfully.
completed. The model list on this host included openai-codex/gpt-5.5 and did not list openai/gpt-5.5.

commands_run.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... mock broker smoke script ... PY
commands_run.result: passed. Started cargo run -q -p autocomplete-server --bin autocomplete-broker with a temporary mock TOML config, waited for /health, then posted 12 alternating synthetic TypeScript and Markdown requests to /v1/autocomplete.

commands_run.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... pi broker smoke script with [pi] command="pi", provider="openai-codex", model="gpt-5.5", thinking="minimal", timeout_ms=10000 ... PY
commands_run.result: passed. Started cargo run -q -p autocomplete-server --bin autocomplete-broker with a temporary pi TOML config, waited for /health, then posted one synthetic TypeScript and one synthetic Markdown request to /v1/autocomplete. Both returned status=ok before the strict timeouts.

commands_run.command: timeout 12s pi --print --mode json --no-tools --no-session --no-context-files --no-extensions --no-skills --no-prompt-templates --no-themes --model openai/gpt-5.5 --thinking minimal --system-prompt 'You are an inline autocomplete provider. Return only valid JSON with keys insert_text, confidence, and optional reason. The insert_text must be exactly the text to insert at the cursor: no markdown fences, no prose, no explanations, and no repeated prefix.' '<synthetic autocomplete request JSON>'
commands_run.result: failed as expected for the plan/default OpenAI-provider path on this host. pi exited with code 1 and reported: No API key found for openai. The bounded command completed in under 12 seconds.

latency_ms.measurement: external HTTP wall clock from Python time.perf_counter; server startup excluded; nearest-rank p95
external HTTP wall clock from Python time.perf_counter; server startup excluded; nearest-rank p95; small n=2 sample

default_plan_model_failure.observed_failure: exit code 1; stdout/stderr included 'No API key found for openai. Use /login to log into a provider via OAuth or API key.'

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Run the Rust broker with mock provider and submit simple TypeScript and Markdown autocomplete requests.
evidence: Mock /health returned status ok and provider mock/available.
12 alternating external HTTP /v1/autocomplete requests returned status ok with postprocessed metadata.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Run the Rust broker with pi provider using strict timeout if practical; record whether pi returns at least one code and one markdown suggestion.
evidence: Broker pi config using provider=openai-codex and model=gpt-5.5 returned one TypeScript suggestion and one Markdown suggestion within 10 s request/provider deadlines.
The plan/default openai/gpt-5.5 path failed due missing OpenAI API key, so the successful smoke used the installed pi model/provider path.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Record median and p95 latency for bounded repeated requests where feasible.
evidence: Mock: n=12, median 0.921 ms, nearest-rank p95 1.939 ms.
pi: n=2, median 5391.979 ms, nearest-rank p95 6254.653 ms; sample is intentionally small to keep real pi calls bounded.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Document failure-mode notes without overstating quality.
evidence: Failure modes and limitations are recorded in failure_mode_notes.
No GUI editor-host behavior or daily-use quality is claimed.

failure_mode_notes.title: Default plan model path failed without OpenAI API key
failure_mode_notes.details: The direct bounded pi command using --model openai/gpt-5.5 exited with code 1 and reported no OpenAI API key. This host's pi --list-models output showed openai-codex/gpt-5.5, which worked when configured explicitly via pi.provider=openai-codex and pi.model=gpt-5.5. Documentation/config should avoid assuming openai/gpt-5.5 is universally usable.

failure_mode_notes.title: Real pi latency is too slow for keystroke-level autocomplete
failure_mode_notes.details: Two successful real broker requests took 4529.306 ms and 6254.653 ms over external HTTP. This supports manual or idle-after-pause MVP smoke only; it does not satisfy Copilot-like per-keystroke latency.

failure_mode_notes.title: Provider identity in health/source omits configured pi.provider
failure_mode_notes.details: The successful broker config set pi.provider=openai-codex and pi.model=gpt-5.5, but /health and response source reported pi:gpt-5.5. This is not a smoke blocker, but clearer diagnostics would include the provider prefix when configured.

failure_mode_notes.title: GUI editor behavior remains source/test-only
failure_mode_notes.details: This task did not run VS Code Extension Development Host or a disposable Obsidian vault. It verifies the broker protocol path with vscode/obsidian client names, not actual editor rendering or Tab acceptance.

failure_mode_notes.title: Small pi sample size limits latency statistics
failure_mode_notes.details: The pi measurement intentionally used only one code and one Markdown request to bound real model calls. Median and p95 are recorded, but n=2 is insufficient for production latency characterization.

safety_notes: Real pi calls used synthetic TypeScript and Markdown contexts only; no repository or sensitive file content was sent.
Broker pi invocation includes --no-tools, --no-session, --no-context-files, --no-extensions, --no-skills, --no-prompt-templates, and --no-themes per the verified provider implementation.
Every real pi attempt had bounded provider/request/client/outer-command timeouts.

This task did not read tmp/, archive/, prompt-buffer.md, or unrelated ~/dev directories. A top-level git status/listing mentioned archive/ as an untracked path; no archive files were opened or edited.

recommended_follow_ups.title: Document pi provider/model selection for subscription-backed runs
recommended_follow_ups.details: MVP-060 docs should show how to set [pi] provider="openai-codex" model="gpt-5.5" when pi --list-models exposes openai-codex models, and should mention the openai/gpt-5.5 API-key failure mode.

recommended_follow_ups.title: Consider including configured pi provider in health/source diagnostics
recommended_follow_ups.details: When pi.provider is set, health/source strings should distinguish pi:openai-codex/gpt-5.5 from pi:gpt-5.5 to make runtime measurements easier to interpret.

worktree_notes: Pre-existing unrelated deleted legacy reports/orchestration/* entries and untracked orchestration/archive paths were visible before this task and were not modified.
